entries.title: US and Iran in blockade standoff as Pakistan pushes for talks
entries.snippet: The mood in the Strait of Hormuz remains combustible despite Trump's ceasefire extension.

entries.title: Billionaire backer sues Trump family's crypto firm over alleged extortion
entries.snippet: Billionaire investor Justin Sun is suing the family's World Liberty crypto venture after spending $45m on its tokens.

entries.title: Pope Leo criticises Equatorial Guinea prisons as he winds up Africa tour
entries.snippet: The Pope gives a frank and passionate speech, the likes of which have characterised his tour of Africa.

entries.title: EU approves €90bn loan for Ukraine as pipeline is turned on ending deadlock
entries.snippet: Ukraine says it has reopened the Druzhba pipeline, after months of stalemate over stalled oil supplies to neighbouring Hungary.

entries.title: Nigeria's suspected coup plotters deny treason charges
entries.snippet: Allegations of a coup plot first surfaced last year when an Independence Day parade was cancelled.

entries.title: Lufthansa cuts 20,000 summer flights as fuel prices surge
entries.snippet: The airline is the latest to cut flights as the US-Israel war with Iran sends jet fuel prices soaring.

entries.title: No cartels involved - but Mexico's pyramid attack prompts new concerns
entries.snippet: The deadly shooting at a popular tourist site weeks before the World Cup has rocked Mexico.

entries.title: US charges anti-extremism organisation over payments to informants in hate groups
entries.snippet: The Southern Poverty Law Center said it would "vigorously defend ourselves, our staff, and our work".

entries.title: South Korean fighter jets collided due to pilots taking pictures, report finds
entries.snippet: One of the pilots has been fined thousands of dollars by the military to cover repair costs.

entries.title: Taiwan president cancels trip after African countries revoke flight permits
entries.snippet: Taiwan has accused Beijing of putting pressure on African countries to close their airspace to Lai's plane.

entries.title: Los Angeles becomes first major US school district to limit classroom screen time
entries.snippet: The school district, which serves about half a million students, is looking to reduce its reliance on devices after the pandemic.

entries.title: UAE-backed Colombian mercenaries provided support to Sudan paramilitary, report says
entries.snippet: The Conflict Insights Group (CIG) says its research also shows the extent of UAE involvement.

entries.title: Trump buys time for Iran deal after frantic day of diplomacy
entries.snippet: The US president's decision marked the second time in as many weeks he has backed off a threat to escalate the war, buying more time.

entries.title: Thirteen killed in second India fireworks blast in three days
entries.snippet: Earlier this week, an explosion at a firecracker factory in neighbouring state Tamil Nadu left at least 25 dead.

entries.title: Meta to track workers' clicks and keystrokes to train AI
entries.snippet: The firm will take data from the way employees work for its artificial intelligence models.

entries.title: From Epstein to sock puppets: Key takeaways from Kevin Warsh's Fed confirmation hearing
entries.snippet: He denied making a deal with Donald Trump on interest rates and backed "policy regime change" at the central bank.

entries.title: Iran sees mass redundancies from war with US and Israel
entries.snippet: The impact is being felt by manufacturers, retailers and the digital sector, amid warnings it could get worse if the war resumes.

entries.title: Virginia approves redistricting measure, boosting Democrats' hopes for midterms
entries.snippet: The new maps approved by the state's voters could allow Democrats to flip as many as four House seats.

entries.title: Israeli soldiers punished over vandalism of Jesus statue in Lebanon
entries.snippet: Two soldiers will be removed from combat duty and receive 30 days of military detention, Israel's military says.

entries.title: US singer D4vd pleads not guilty to murder in death of missing teen girl
entries.snippet: Prosecutors say the singer, real name David Anthony Burke, could face life in prison due to the nature of the case, if convicted.

entries.title: Chemical leak at a W.Va. plant kills 2 people, sends 30 more to hospitals, officials say
entries.snippet: The leak occurred at the Catalyst Refiners plant, a silver recovery business. An emergency management official says workers were preparing to shut down at least part of the facility when the leak occurred, causing a chemical gas reaction.

entries.title: Pentagon says Navy secretary is leaving, the latest departure of a top defense leader
entries.snippet: Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell said John Phelan, the Navy's top civilian official, was "departing the administration, effective immediately." Navy Undersecretary Hung Cao will become acting secretary of the Navy.

entries.title: Tesla's profits beat expectations, but Elon Musk says big costs are ahead
entries.snippet: Tesla's profits were up from this time last year. But the company warned investors to prepare for expensive investments in next-generation technology like humanoid robots and AI.

entries.title: The tariff refund process has begun for businesses. What about customers?
entries.snippet: While shipping companies are pledging refunds for customers who directly paid tariff fees, the situation is much trickier for retailers.

entries.title: Inside Linda McMahon's effort to dismantle the Department of Education
entries.snippet: A former pro-wrestling executive, McMahon is now the education secretary Trump tasked with abolishing the agency. New Yorker writer Zach Helfand explains how her WWE experience led her to this role.

entries.title: Iranians are leaving the country just to access the internet
entries.snippet: Iran has cut off its access to the global internet. To find an internet connection, some Iranians are traveling across the border with Turkey — even just to make video calls and then go back home.

entries.title: Gene therapy for a rare type of deafness shows lasting results
entries.snippet: Researchers say a gene therapy allowed deaf children and adults as old as 32 to hear for the first time. The benefits have persisted for more than two years for some patients.

entries.title: Family influencers make the lifestyle look good. But kids pay the price, new book says
entries.snippet: What does it mean to monetize your offspring? To turn their childhood into content? In Like, Follow, Subscribe Fortesa Latifi explores what drives parents to become family influencers.

entries.title: Greetings from an Islamabad park, a peaceful vantage point in an uncertain world
entries.snippet: The park, near the venue where inconclusive Iran-U.S. peace talks took place this month, provides respite to those who visit it.

entries.title: Restrictions on obesity drug coverage force patients to pivot
entries.snippet: Twelve million people lost coverage for Zepbound over the last year. The same number of people lost coverage for Wegovy, according to an analysis by GoodRx, a drug discount website.

entries.title: UK Parliament passes bill that will ban cigarette purchases for younger generations
entries.snippet: Children born after Dec. 31, 2008 will be banned from ever buying cigarettes under the Tobacco and Vapes Bill.

entries.title: Searchers find body of 1 of 6 missing crew from U.S.-registered ship hit by Typhoon Sinlaku
entries.snippet: U.S. Air Force divers used an underwater drone on Tuesday to search inside the overturned ship, the U.S. Coast Guard said in a news release. Divers from Japan's coast guard further examined the ship, called the Mariana, but didn't find the other five, it said.

entries.title: Trump Media replaces Nunes as Truth Social CEO after stock plunge that wiped out billions
entries.snippet: The Trump business behind Truth Social is replacing a former congressman and big supporter of the U.S. president as the leader of the social media platform after a stock collapse that wiped out billions in investor wealth.

entries.title: Pope Leo calls for better prison conditions during Equatorial Guinea visit at end of Africa tour
entries.snippet: Leo's stop, at the end of his four-nation African tour, took on added significance after it emerged that Equatorial Guinea was one of several African nations that have been paid millions of dollars in controversial deals with the Trump administration to receive migrants deported

entries.title: 4 takeaways from Health Secretary RFK Jr.'s gauntlet of congressional hearings
entries.snippet: In his testimony to various committees in the Senate and the House over multiple days, Kennedy was tasked with defending President Donald Trump's proposed 2027 budget, which would boost defense spending while cutting more than 12% of funding from the Department of Health and Huma

entries.title: Iran targets ships in Strait of Hormuz as U.S. talks remain uncertain
entries.snippet: Iranian forces attacked and seized at least two ships in the contested Strait of Hormuz as a standoff over when or whether to return to negotiations continued. The Trump administration said it did not consider the attacks violations of a ceasefire that President Trump extended on

entries.title: Hormuz standoff the 'largest supply shock' ever experienced, says global energy expert
entries.snippet: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has come to a virtual standstill since the U.S. and Israel attacked Iran almost two months ago. Around 20% of the world's petrochemical production normally flows through the strait, and the ripple effects of the drastic cut grow daily. To expl

entries.title: News Wrap: Virginia voters approve new congressional map that could help Democrats
entries.snippet: In our news wrap Wednesday, Democrats are celebrating a win in Virginia, where voters approved a plan to redraw the state's congressional map in a way that could help them pick up four seats in the midterms, the Supreme Court found that an Army veteran who was injured by a suicid

entries.title: RFK Jr. defends vaccine guidance changes and health spending cuts
entries.snippet: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. was back testifying on Capitol Hill, giving members of Congress a chance to press him on some of the biggest changes he's making on spending cuts, vaccines and other public health issues. Lisa Desjardins reports.

entries.title: CBP officer charged with assault over treatment of protester outside Colorado ICE facility
entries.snippet: An immigration officer has been charged with third-degree assault and criminal mischief following an investigation into how he treated a protester in Durango, Colorado, who said the officer put her in a chokehold.

entries.title: Deaths of Americans raise questions about U.S. operations against cartels in Mexico
entries.snippet: An investigation is underway after four officials, including two Americans, were killed in a car crash in Mexico. It's being called an accident by the local government, but it happened after an operation to destroy drug labs in a mountainous area. It's been widely reported that t

entries.title: Uganda sees spike in disease-related deaths after elimination of USAID
entries.snippet: In 2025, the Trump administration dissolved the $40 billion U.S. Agency for International Development, or USAID. Days later, an exemption for "life-saving humanitarian assistance" was issued. But what that included was not specified and aid for health programs has been drasticall

entries.title: Election officials sound alarm over political interference in midterms
entries.snippet: After Democrats won an election in Virginia to redraw congressional maps in their favor, President Trump called it, without evidence, a "rigged election" and "travesty of justice." It's the latest example of Trump casting doubt on the U.S. election process. Liz Landers reports on

entries.title: National Park Foundation chief on protecting America's shared spaces
entries.snippet: In 2025, national parks recorded more than 323 million visits, and 26 parks set attendance records. But as the parks gain popularity, the Trump administration has proposed slashing $1 billion from the National Park Service, likely eliminating thousands of jobs from an already und

entries.title: Mexico to boost security at tourist sites ahead of World Cup after shooting at pyramids
entries.snippet: The Monday shooting, carried out by a lone gunman on top of one of the Teotihuacan pyramids — a UNESCO Heritage Site and one of Mexico's most frequented tourist attractions — killed one Canadian tourist and injured a dozen more.

entries.title: Navy Secretary John Phelan is leaving, Pentagon says, in latest departure of a top defense leader
entries.snippet: In a statement posted to social media, Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell said Phelan was "departing the administration, effective immediately."

entries.title: WATCH: Leavitt says Trump has not set 'firm deadline' for Iranian proposal to extend ceasefire
entries.snippet: "Ultimately, the timeline would be dictated by the commander in chief and the president of the United States," Leavitt said.

entries.title: Federal appeals court blocks California law requiring federal agents to wear identification
entries.snippet: California lawyers argued that the law applied equally to all law enforcement officers without discriminating against the U.S. government, and that it was important for public safety.

entries.title: Supreme Court revives wounded veteran's lawsuit against a contractor over suicide bombing
entries.snippet: The Supreme Court on Wednesday cleared the way for a veteran wounded by a suicide bomb in Afghanistan to sue the government contractor for whom the attacker was working when he built the explosive.

entries.title: Wildfires destroy nearly 50 homes in Georgia as blazes grow in Florida
entries.snippet: Some of the biggest blazes were along Georgia's coast and between and around Jacksonville, Florida, a state that is facing one of its worst fire seasons in decades.

entries.title: DW
entries.snippet: News, Analysis and Service from Germany and Europe - in 30 Languages

entries.title: Germany's Merz: Climate protection must not hold economy back
entries.snippet: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said the public would not accept climate policies that lead to deindustrialization, warning that they would hinder progress and innovation.

entries.title: School's out in Iran: No phone, no internet, no classes
entries.snippet: Iran's schools are closed because of the war. Classes are being held via TV programs and the national intranet. Children without a laptop or smartphone are left out. The country is effectively cut off from the internet.

entries.title: EU proposes steps to deal with energy crisis amid Iran war
entries.snippet: Brussels has unveiled a toolbox to manage energy shocks, as concerns grow over fuel supplies and rising costs. It includes a push to decrease the bloc's reliance on fossil fuels.

entries.title: Japan: South Korean arrested outside Yasukuni Shrine ceremony for war dead
entries.snippet: Police said a South Korean national holding a banner with political messages was arrested at Tokyo's Yasukuni Shrine to Japan's war dead. Japan's prime minister did not attend proceedings, but around 120 lawmakers did.

entries.title: Pope Leo decries inequality in corrupt Equatorial Guinea
entries.snippet: Pope Leo XIV continued his tour of Africa with a Mass in Equatorial Guinea. The pontiff called for more justice in the country as the nation's president and his family looked on.

entries.title: Germany's finance minister wants to scrap spousal tax splitting
entries.snippet: Highly controversial, but not well understood: German Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil wants to change the tax benefits for married couples.

entries.title: Nepal: Interior minister elected on anti-corruption platform steps down amid financial investigation
entries.snippet: Nepal's new government elected on an anti-corruption platform after youth protests toppled its predecessor has now lost two ministers in weeks. Sudan Gurung said he was quitting to ensure "a fair investigation."

entries.title: ICC blocks ex-Philippine President Duterte release, says it can try him
entries.snippet: Judges ruled the court has authority to try the former Philippine leader despite the Philippines' exit from the ICC.

entries.title: Iran war fuels crisis for Africa's aviation sector
entries.snippet: The Iran war is pushing up jet fuel prices, forcing African airlines to raise ticket prices, cancel flights and rethink operations to stay financially viable.

entries.title: Russia to block Kazakh oil flows on key pipeline supplying Berlin
entries.snippet: Germany has confirmed that Russia will block flows of Kazakh oil to a vital refinery that supplies much of Berlin's energy needs from May 1.

entries.title: EU unblocks €90 billion loan to Ukraine
entries.snippet: Envoys from EU member states have finally paved the way for a €90 billion loan to Ukraine that had long been blocked by Hungary. They also approved a 20th package of sanctions on Russia over its invasion of its neighbor.

entries.title: The rise and fall of East Germany's Palace of the Republic
entries.snippet: From 1976 through 1990, the building in East Berlin served as a symbol of the communist state's power. The "People's Palace" was controversially destroyed — and yet remains present.

entries.title: Ukraine says Russian oil to Hungary, Slovakia has restarted
entries.snippet: Energy companies in Ukraine and Hungary have both said that oil flows through the Druzhba pipeline from Russia via Belarus have restarted. Hungary and Slovakia expect the first supplies to arrive by Thursday.

entries.title: Iran war: Revolutionary Guard says seizes ships in Hormuz
entries.snippet: Iran's naval forces accused two ships of endangering maritime security by attempting to cross the Strait of Hormuz without authorization. The escalation comes amid uncertainty over US-Iran ceasefire talks. DW has more.

entries.title: Israeli ambassador: 'Only a strong Israel can achieve peace'
entries.snippet: In an interview with DW, Ron Prosor, Israel's ambassador to Germany, defends his country's military strikes against Iran and Lebanon. He also advises Germany's Chancellor Friedrich Merz to show more "tact."

entries.title: Avoid men, German police association chief tells women
entries.snippet: Police association chief Dirk Peglow has stirred debate with his advice to women to avoid relationships with men for safety's sake. But his provocative tip is not without reason, as statistics show.

entries.title: Will Iran war reshape global trade more than COVID?
entries.snippet: As the Iran war simmers, trade experts wonder if this conflict will reshape supply chains more than the pandemic. Companies are already rethinking strategies to future-proof revenues from other geopolitical risks.

entries.title: Germany news: Lufthansa scraps 20,000 flights
entries.snippet: As Frankfurt Airport opens a new terminal, Lufthansa has scrapped 20,000 flights due to soaring fuel prices. Meanwhile, the Economy Ministry has reduced its forecast growth down to 0.5%. DW has more.

entries.title: Iran war: Jet fuel crisis deepens as Lufthansa cuts flights
entries.snippet: The impact of jet fuel shortages because of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz is starting to burn. Lufthansa has canceled 20,000 flights and experts warn more cancellations and price hikes are on the way.
